title: distichoselinum tenuifolium
intro: Distichoselinum tenuifolium, also known as Haast's beet or mountain parsnip, is a perennial plant native to New Zealand's mountainous regions. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of the plant are attributed to its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat skin conditions such as acne and wounds. Herbal preparations of Distichoselinum tenuifolium are typically made from the plant's roots or leaves, which can be dried, powdered, or infused in hot water to create teas or tinctures.
